rules.rule: Focus on one technical point per session only.
rules.reason: Beginners cannot process multiple corrections simultaneously. One clear cue leads to faster improvement.

rules.rule: Practise at 50% speed until the movement feels natural, then gradually increase pace.
rules.reason: Motor patterns are built through repetition at manageable speeds. Speed before technique leads to bad habits.

rules.rule: 200+ repetitions of the same skill per session before moving to a new drill.
rules.reason: Beginners need volume of the same movement to build a motor pattern. Variety too early confuses muscle memory.

rules.rule: Always identify one thing the player did well before correcting anything.
rules.reason: Confidence is a beginner's most fragile asset. Positive reinforcement keeps them engaged.

rules.rule: Never use technical terms without immediately explaining them in plain language.
rules.reason: Technical jargon creates distance between the player and the instruction. Plain language = faster understanding.
